title: Stabilize TSX data contracts for the dashboard API
summary: Refined the frontend data contract so the API route and TSX shell agree on sessions, conflicts, stats, and peer metadata without repeated reshaping in components.
goal: Reduce frontend glue code by giving the route and the React shell a cleaner contract for session-driven dashboards.
changesMade: Normalized session and conflict payload shapes
Added explicit stats for counts the shell reads repeatedly
Consolidated file conflict derivation inside the loader layer
Removed assumptions from the TSX shell about missing branch and status fields

features.name: dashboard data normalization
features.why: TSX components should spend time rendering, not guessing at missing backend fields.

features.name: frontend stats payload
features.why: The shell header and future summary widgets need counts without recomputing everything client-side.

features.name: conflict payload alignment
features.why: The conflict drawer depends on a stable set of session titles and paths.

decisions.decision: Keep file-based reading inside lib/data.ts for now
decisions.why: The dashboard is still repo-local, and the route should not open files directly in multiple places.

decisions.decision: Return stats from the route instead of deriving them in the shell
decisions.why: The same counts are used in multiple places and should stay consistent.

openQuestions: Do we want one combined payload for the full shell or smaller route slices once the UI becomes interactive?
resumePrompt: Continue data contract cleanup in dashboard/src/lib/data.ts, then keep dashboard/src/app/api/data/route.ts as the single place that assembles shell payloads.
